resources.string: Automatically launch Link Opener when you start your computer to keep it ready in the system tray.
This browser is already in the list.
Can't add Link Opener as a browser.
No browsers available — discover or add some first.
No rules yet. Click "+ Add rule…" to make every link to a domain open in a specific browser.
When off, multi-profile browsers like Chrome show as one row. Rules and exclusions referencing specific profiles will not match while disabled.
When on, a close button appears in the Settings window header. The window can always be closed via the system title bar.
Link Opener isn't your default browser
Set Link Opener as the default to intercept links opened from any app.
Link Opener is currently the default browser.
Link Opener is not the default browser.
How to make Link Opener your default
Until Link Opener is installed via a packaged build, the OS will not list it as a browser candidate. You can prepare the steps now and finish them after the next packaging release.
If the status didn't update right away, hit the refresh button in the top-right corner.
This operating system isn't supported yet.

string-array.name: default_browser_instructions_macos
string-array.item: Click the button below to open System Settings → Desktop & Dock.
Scroll to the "Default web browser" dropdown.
Pick Link Opener from the list.

string-array.name: default_browser_instructions_windows
string-array.item: Click the button below to open Settings → Apps → Default apps.
Find "Web browser" in the list of categories.
Click the current default and select Link Opener.

string-array.name: default_browser_instructions_linux
string-array.item: Or use your distribution's default-applications manager (e.g., GNOME Control Center → Default Applications).

resources.string: No browsers detected on this system.
Open Settings to manage exclusions or install a browser.
Already here. I heard you the first time.
Have you tried turning it off and on again?
Close settings without quitting Link Opener

Two extremely cool guys™ and Claude Code spent a hackathon putting this together.

Runs on a strict diet of vibes, prayers, and copious caffeine. Mileage may vary. Performance not guaranteed past sunset.

Found a bug? Three options:

→ Open a [Pull Request](https://github.com/mazunin-v-jb/kmp-link-opener) — bring fixes
→ File an [Issue](https://github.com/mazunin-v-jb/kmp-link-opener/issues) — bring problems
→ [Light a candle](https://chapel-online.com/category/postavit-svechku/) — for the app's repose, or its swift recovery. Whichever fits the situation.

string-array.name: default_browser_instructions_android
string-array.item: Tap the button below — Android will show a system dialog asking if you want Link Opener as your default browser.
If the dialog doesn\'t appear (older Android), it will open Settings → Apps → Default apps instead.
Pick Link Opener under the "Browser app" entry.
